root_text.an4.103:1.8: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro kumbhūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an4.103:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo tuccho hoti pihito?
root_text.an4.103:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa pāsādikaṁ hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.103:2.3: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.103:2.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo tuccho hoti pihito.
root_text.an4.103:2.5: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, kumbho tuccho pihito;
root_text.an4.103:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo pūro hoti vivaṭo?
root_text.an4.103:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa na pāsādikaṁ hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.103:3.3: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.103:3.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo pūro hoti vivaṭo.
root_text.an4.103:3.5: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, kumbho pūro vivaṭo;
root_text.an4.103:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo tuccho hoti vivaṭo?
root_text.an4.103:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa na pāsādikaṁ hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.103:4.3: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an4.103:4.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo tuccho hoti vivaṭo.
root_text.an4.103:4.5: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, kumbho tuccho vivaṭo;
root_text.an4.103:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, puggalo pūro hoti pihito?
root_text.an4.103:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa puggalassa pāsādikaṁ hoti abhikkantaṁ paṭikkantaṁ ālokitaṁ vilokitaṁ samiñjitaṁ pasāritaṁ saṅghāṭipattacīvaradhāraṇaṁ.
root_text.an4.103:5.3: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an4.103:5.4: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, puggalo pūro hoti pihito.
root_text.an4.103:5.5: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, kumbho pūro pihito;
root_text.an4.103:5.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro kumbhūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an4.103:1.8: In the same way, these four individuals similar to pots are found in the world.
translation_text.an4.103:2.1: And how is a individual covered but hollow?
translation_text.an4.103:2.2: It’s when an individual is impressive when going out and coming back, when looking ahead and aside, when bending and extending the limbs, and when bearing the outer robe, bowl and robes.
translation_text.an4.103:2.3: But they don’t truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an4.103:2.4: That’s how an individual is covered but hollow.
translation_text.an4.103:2.5: That individual is like a pot that’s covered but hollow, I say.
translation_text.an4.103:3.1: And how is a individual uncovered but full?
translation_text.an4.103:3.2: It’s when an individual is not impressive …
translation_text.an4.103:3.3: But they truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.103:4.1: And how is a individual uncovered and hollow?
translation_text.an4.103:4.2: It’s when an individual is not impressive …
translation_text.an4.103:4.3: Nor do they truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.103:5.1: And how is a individual covered and full?
translation_text.an4.103:5.2: It’s when an individual is impressive …
translation_text.an4.103:5.3: And they truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ …
translation_text.an4.103:5.7: These four individuals similar to pots are found in the world.”
